00483| 1. **Pre-impl exploration:** Explore codebase with Task tool before coding
00484| 2. **TDD Cycle:** RED (write failing test) → GREEN (minimal code to pass) → REFACTOR
00485| 3. **UI features:** Mandatory smoke test (tests passing ≠ feature working)
00486| 4. **Commit pattern:** Separate test and implementation commits
00487| 
00488| **Red flags (STOP and restart):**
00489| - Writing code before test
00490| - Test passes immediately
00491| - Rationalizing "just this once"
00492| 
00493| **Completion:** All tests pass, reported via `bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: Validation"`
00494| 
00495| **Reference:** See `~/.claude/skills/worker/feature-impl/reference/phase-implementation-tdd.md` and `reference/tdd-best-practices.md`.
00496| 
00497| ---
00498| 
00499| ### Implementation Phase (Direct Mode)
00500| 
00501| **Purpose:** Implement non-behavioral changes without TDD overhead.
00502| 
00503| **When to use:** Refactoring, configuration, documentation, code cleanup.
00504| 
00505| ⚠️ **Critical:** If changing behavior → STOP and switch to TDD mode.
00506| 
00507| **Key workflow:**
00508| 1. **Pre-impl exploration:** Verify change is non-behavioral
00509| 2. Run existing tests (establish baseline)
00510| 3. Make focused changes (≤2 files, ≤1 hour)
00511| 4. Verify no regressions
00512| 
00513| **Completion:** Tests pass, reported via `bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: Validation"`
00514| 
00515| **Reference:** See `~/.claude/skills/worker/feature-impl/reference/phase-implementation-direct.md`.
00516| 
00517| ---
00518| 
00519| ### Validation Phase
00520| 
00521| **Purpose:** Verify implementation works as intended.
00522| 
00523| **Validation levels:**
00524| 
00525| | Level | Workflow |
00526| |-------|----------|
00527| | `none` | Commit, report complete |
00528| | `tests` | Run test suite, verify pass, commit |
00529| | `smoke-test` | Tests + manual verification + evidence capture |
00530| | `multi-phase` | Tests + smoke + STOP for orchestrator approval |
00531| 
00532| **⚠️ UI Visual Verification (MANDATORY if web/ files modified):**
00533| 
00534| Before completing, run: `git diff --name-only | grep "^web/"`
00535| 
00536| If ANY files returned → Visual verification is REQUIRED:
00537| 1. Rebuild server: `make install` then restart via `orch servers`
00538| 2. Capture screenshot via Playwright MCP (`browser_take_screenshot` tool)
00539| 3. Document evidence: `bd comment <beads-id> "Visual verification: [description]"`
00540| 
00541| **⛔ Cannot mark Phase: Complete without visual evidence for web/ changes.**
00542| 
00543| **When validation fails:**
00544| 1. Check logs for runtime errors (test output, project logs)
00545| 2. Analyze failure output
00546| 3. Return to Implementation, fix, re-validate
00547| 
00548| **Completion:** Validation evidence documented, reported via `bd comment`
00549| 
00550| **Reference:** See `~/.claude/skills/worker/feature-impl/reference/phase-validation.md` and `reference/validation-examples.md`.
00551| 
00552| ---
00553| 
00554| ### Integration Phase
00555| 
00556| **Purpose:** Combine multiple validated phases into cohesive feature.
00557| 
00558| **When to use:** Multi-phase features after individual phases validated.
00559| 
00560| **Key workflow:**
00561| 1. Review all completed phases via beads history
00562| 2. Identify integration points (data flow, shared state, API contracts)
00563| 3. Write integration tests for cross-phase scenarios
00564| 4. E2E verification + regression testing
00565| 5. Final smoke test of complete feature
00566| 
00567| **Completion:** Integration tests pass, reported via `bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: Validation"`
00568| 
00569| **Reference:** See `~/.claude/skills/worker/feature-impl/reference/phase-integration.md`.
00570| 
00571| ---
00572| 
00573| ## Self-Review Phase (REQUIRED)
00574| 
00575| **Purpose:** Quality gate before completion.
00576| 
00577| **Perform these checks before marking complete:**
00578| 
00579| ### Original Symptom Validation (For Bug Fixes)
00580| - [ ] Re-ran the EXACT command/scenario from original issue (same flags, same mode)
00581| - [ ] Documented actual result (not an estimate) via `bd comment`
00582| - [ ] If testing different mode/flag than original, explicitly justified why
00583| 
00584| **⚠️ Scope Redefinition Warning:** Agents can claim "fix complete" by testing a different scenario (e.g., `--json` flag when issue showed bare command). The fix is only verified when the original failing scenario passes.
00585| 
00586| ### Anti-Pattern Detection
00587| - [ ] No god objects (files >300 lines or multiple concerns)
00588| - [ ] No tight coupling (use dependency injection)
00589| - [ ] No magic values (use named constants)
00590| - [ ] No deep nesting (>3 levels → extract to helpers)
00591| - [ ] No incomplete work (TODOs, placeholders)
00592| 
00593| ### Security Review
00594| - [ ] No hardcoded secrets
00595| - [ ] No injection vulnerabilities (SQL, XSS, command, path traversal)
00596| 
00597| ### Commit Hygiene
00598| - [ ] Conventional format: `type: description`
00599| - [ ] Atomic commits (one logical change each)
00600| - [ ] No WIP commits
00601| 
00602| ### Test Coverage
00603| - [ ] Happy path tested
00604| - [ ] Edge cases covered
00605| - [ ] Error paths tested
00606| 
00607| ### Documentation
00608| - [ ] Public APIs documented
00609| - [ ] No commented-out code or debug statements
00610| 
00611| ### Deliverables
00612| - [ ] All required deliverables exist and complete
00613| - [ ] Deliverables reported via `bd comment`
00614| 
00615| ### Integration Wiring (CRITICAL)
00616| - [ ] New modules imported somewhere
00617| - [ ] New functions called somewhere
00618| - [ ] New routes registered
00619| - [ ] New components rendered
00620| - [ ] No orphaned code
00621| 
00622| ### Demo Data Ban (CRITICAL)
00623| - [ ] No fake identities in production code
00624| - [ ] No placeholder domains (use env vars)
00625| - [ ] No lorem ipsum or magic numbers as data
00626| 
00627| ### Scope Verification (For refactoring/migration)
00628| - [ ] Ran `rg "old_pattern"` - should return 0
00629| - [ ] Ran `rg "new_pattern"` - should match expected count
00630| 
00631| ### Discovered Work
00632| - [ ] Reviewed for discoveries (bugs, tech debt, enhancement ideas)
00633| - [ ] Created beads issues with `triage:review` label (default - lets orchestrator review before daemon spawns)
00634| 
00635| ### Original Symptom Validation (For Bug Fixes)
00636| 
00637| ⚠️ **This gate is MANDATORY for bug fixes.** Skip only for pure features/refactoring.
00638| 
00639| **Purpose:** Prevent "scope redefinition" - fixing a different problem than the original symptom.
00640| 
00641| **Before marking complete:**
00642| 1. **Re-run the original failing command** from the issue
00643|    - Not a similar command - the EXACT command (same flags, same mode)
00644|    - Example: If issue shows `time orch status # 1:25.67`, run `time orch status` (not `time orch status --json`)
00645| 2. **Document the actual result** in a beads comment:
00646|    ```bash
00647|    bd comment <beads-id> "Original symptom validation: [command] → [result]"
00648|    ```
00649| 3. **Compare against original evidence** - is the symptom resolved?
00650| 
00651| **⚠️ Scope Redefinition Warning:**
00652| If your fix validates against a DIFFERENT command/mode than the original issue:
00653| - Example: Original issue shows text mode slow, you're testing JSON mode
00654| - Example: Original issue shows `--verbose` flag, you're testing without it
00655| 
00656| This is a RED FLAG. Either:
00657| - Re-test with the original command and document result
00658| - OR explicitly justify why the different command is a valid proxy (with beads comment)
00659| 
00660| **Checklist:**
00661| - [ ] Re-ran exact original command from issue
00662| - [ ] Documented actual timing/behavior via `bd comment`
00663| - [ ] Result matches expected fix (not an estimate like "~10s")
00664| - [ ] If testing different mode/flags: justified why via `bd comment`
00665| 
00666| **If issues found:** Fix immediately, commit, re-verify.
00667| 
00668| **If validation skipped:** Document why in completion comment (e.g., "Original symptom validation: N/A - pure refactoring, no bug fix")
00669| 
00670| **Reference:** `.kb/investigations/2025-12-29-inv-root-cause-analysis-agent-orch.md` - Root cause analysis showing why this gate matters.
00671| 
00672| **If issues found:** Fix immediately, commit, re-verify.
00673| 
00674| **When passed:** `bd comment <beads-id> "Self-review passed - ready for completion"`
00675| 
00676| ---
00677| 
00678| ## Leave it Better (REQUIRED)
00679| 
00680| **Purpose:** Every session should externalize what you learned.

00682| **Before marking complete, run at least one:**

00688| | Found a constraint | `kb quick constrain "X" --reason "Y"` |
00689| | Open question | `kb quick question "X"` |

00695| - [ ] Ran at least one `kb quick` command OR noted why nothing to externalize
00696| - [ ] Included "Leave it Better" status in completion comment

00703| 1. Report progress: `bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: <new-phase> - <brief summary>"`
00704| 2. Output: "Phase complete, moving to next phase"

state.title: .kb/models/dashboard-agent-status.md
state.output:
<file>
00001| # Model: Dashboard Agent Status Calculation
00002| 
00003| **Domain:** Dashboard / Agent Lifecycle
00004| **Last Updated:** 2026-01-12
00005| **Synthesized From:** 11 investigations (Jan 4-8, 2026) into completion verification, status display, and registry behavior
00006| 
00007| ---
00008| 
00009| ## Summary (30 seconds)
00010| 
00011| The dashboard calculates agent status through a **Priority Cascade model**: check Phase first (highest priority), then registry state, then session existence. Status can be "wrong" at the dashboard level while being "correct" at each individual check - this is a measurement artifact, not a system failure. The root issue was observation infrastructure (missing events, double-counting metrics, state not surfaced), not broken completion logic.
00012| 
00013| ---
00014| 
00015| ## Core Mechanism
00016| 
00017| ### How Status Is Calculated
00018| 
00019| **Priority Cascade (Jan 8, 2026):**
00020| ```
00021| 1. Phase check (highest priority)
00022|    - If Phase: Complete → status = completed
00023| 
00024| 2. Registry state check
00025|    - If Status: abandoned → status = abandoned
00026|    - If Status: running → status = running
00027| 
00028| 3. Session existence check (lowest priority)
00029|    - If session doesn't exist → status = dead
00030|    - If session exists → status = running
00031| ```
00032| 
00033| **Why cascade order matters:**
00034| - Phase is canonical (agent's own declaration)
00035| - Registry is authoritative (human-set via `orch abandon`)
00036| - Session existence is fallback (infrastructure check)
00037| 
00038| ### State Transitions
00039| 
00040| **Normal lifecycle:**
00041| ```
00042| spawned (registry + session created)
00043|     ↓
00044| running (working toward Phase: Complete)
00045|     ↓
00046| Phase: Complete reached (agent declares done)
00047|     ↓
00048| orch complete runs (verification + beads close)
00049|     ↓
00050| completed (registry marked, session may remain)
00051| ```
00052| 
00053| **Abandoned path:**
00054| ```
00055| spawned → running
00056|     ↓
00057| orch abandon (human judgment)
00058|     ↓
00059| Status: abandoned in registry
00060|     ↓
00061| Dashboard shows abandoned (override Phase)
00062| ```
00063| 
00064| ### Critical Invariants
00065| 
00066| 1. **Phase: Complete is agent's declaration** - Only agent can reach this, not orchestrator
00067| 2. **Registry is source of truth for abandonment** - Human judgment, can't be inferred
00068| 3. **Session may outlive completion** - Session existence ≠ agent still working
00069| 4. **Status checks don't mutate state** - Calculation is read-only, no side effects
00070| 
00071| ---
00072| 
00073| ## Why This Fails
00074| 
00075| ### Failure Mode 1: "Dead" Agents That Completed
00076| 
00077| **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "dead", but work is done and beads issue closed
00078| 
00079| **Root cause:** Session cleanup happens async, dashboard checks session existence as fallback
00080| 
00081| **Why it happens:**
00082| - Agent reaches Phase: Complete
00083| - `orch complete` verifies and closes beads issue
00084| - Session cleanup happens later (or not at all)
00085| - Dashboard cascade reaches session check → sees no session → "dead"
00086| 
00087| **Fix (Jan 8):** Priority Cascade puts Phase check before session check
00088| 
00089| ### Failure Mode 2: Metrics Show Wrong Completion Rate
00090| 
00091| **Symptom:** `orch stats` showed 72% completion when reality was 89%
00092| 
00093| **Root cause:** Metrics counted events (double-counting) instead of deduplicating entities
00094| 
00095| **Why it happens:**
00096| - `agent.completed` event emitted by both `orch complete` AND beads close hook
00097| - Metrics counted events → some completions counted 2x
00098| - Result: inflated total, deflated completion %
00099| 
00100| **Fix (Jan 8):** Metrics deduplicate by beads_id before calculating ratios
00101| 
00102| ### Failure Mode 3: Work Completed via Bypass Paths
00103| 
00104| **Symptom:** Beads issue closed but no completion event, dashboard doesn't update
00105| 
00106| **Root cause:** `bd close` (direct) doesn't emit events, only `orch complete` does
00107| 
00108| **Why it happens:**
00109| - Multiple paths to completion: `orch complete`, `bd close`, `bd sync` with commit message
00110| - Only `orch complete` emits events
00111| - Other paths are invisible to observation infrastructure
00112| 
00113| **Fix (Jan 6):** Beads close hook emits `agent.completed` event
00114| 
00115| ---
00116| 
00117| ## Constraints
00118| 
00119| ### Why Can't We Just Poll Session State?
00120| 
00121| **Constraint:** OpenCode server doesn't expose session state via HTTP API
00122| 
00123| **Implication:** Dashboard can't ask "is session actually working?" - can only check existence
00124| 
00125| **Workaround:** SSE events for real-time updates, but dashboard refresh is pull-based
00126| 
00127| **This enables:** Simple API design without exposing internal session state
00128| **This constrains:** Dashboard must infer status from existence, not actual execution state
00129| 
00130| ### Why Can't Agents Mark Themselves Complete in Registry?
00131| 
00132| **Constraint:** Agents don't have write access to `~/.orch/registry.json`
00133| 
00134| **Implication:** Only `orch complete` (orchestrator tool) can update registry
00135| 
00136| **Workaround:** Agent declares Phase, orchestrator verifies and records
00137| 
00138| **This enables:** Separation of concerns (agents declare, orchestrator verifies)
00139| **This constrains:** Agents cannot self-terminate or update their own registry state
00140| 
00141| ### Why Not Single Source of Truth?
00142| 
00143| **Constraint:** Three systems with different responsibilities:
00144| - **Agent workspace** (Phase in .phase file) - agent's declaration
00145| - **Registry** (~/.orch/registry.json) - orchestrator's record
00146| - **Beads** (.beads/issues.jsonl) - work tracking
00147| 
00148| **Implication:** Status is computed by combining sources, not stored in one place
00149| 
00150| **Workaround:** Dashboard reconciles across systems
00151| 
00152| **This enables:** Each system optimized for its purpose (declaration, tracking, work management)
00153| **This constrains:** Cannot query single location for definitive status
00154| 
00155| ---
00156| 
00157| ## Evolution
00158| 
00159| ### Jan 4-6, 2026: Discovery Phase
00160| - 8 investigations into "why agents show dead when they're done"
00161| - Hypothesis: Completion verification broken
00162| - Reality: Observation infrastructure gaps (no events, wrong metrics)
00163| 
00164| ### Jan 8, 2026: Priority Cascade Model
00165| - Investigation `2026-01-04-design-dashboard-agent-status-model.md` proposed cascade
00166| - Tested against known failures, explained all observed behavior
00167| - Decision: Implement Priority Cascade, fix event emission gaps
00168| 
00169| ### Jan 12, 2026: Model Synthesis
00170| - 11 investigations synthesized into this model
00171| - Recognition: 89% completion rate, not 72% (measurement artifact)
00172| - Principle emerged: Observation Infrastructure ("if system can't observe it, system can't manage it")
00173| 
00174| ---
00175| 
00176| ## References
00177| 
00178| **Key Investigations:**
00179| - `2026-01-04-design-dashboard-agent-status-model.md` - Priority Cascade design
00180| - `2026-01-08-inv-dashboard-shows-agents-dead-actually.md` - Discovery of 89% actual completion
00181| - `2026-01-08-inv-orch-stats-completion-rate-miscalculated.md` - Metrics double-counting
00182| - `2026-01-06-inv-bd-close-bypass-event-emission.md` - Beads hook gap
00183| - ...and 7 others
00184| 
00185| **Decisions Informed by This Model:**
00186| - `2026-01-08-observation-infrastructure-principle.md` - New principle from this work
00187| - `2026-01-08-priority-cascade-model.md` - Status calculation approach
00188| 
00189| **Related Models:**
00190| - (Future) `agent-lifecycle.md` - Broader lifecycle model
00191| - (Future) `event-emission-architecture.md` - How events flow through system
00192| 
00193| **Primary Evidence (Verify These):**
00194| - `pkg/status/calculate.go` - Status calculation implementation
00195| - `pkg/dashboard/server.go` - Dashboard data serving
00196| - `.beads/hooks/on_close` - Event emission on beads close
00197| 

(End of file - total 197 lines)
</file>

00033| 		},

00038| // This is a blocking call that reads events until the connection is closed.
00039| func (c *SSEClient) Connect(events chan<- SSEEvent) error {
00040| 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Get(c.URL)
00041| 	if err != nil {

00043| 	}

00050| // This is useful for testing with mock readers.
00051| func ReadSSEStream(r io.Reader, events chan<- SSEEvent) error {
00052| 	reader := bufio.NewReader(r)
00053| 	var eventBuffer strings.Builder
00054|

00644| function handleSSEEvent(data: any) {

00650| 	// message.part: text streaming (incremental text chunks)
00651| 	// message.part.updated: tool state changes (running -> completed)
00652| 	if ((data.type === 'message.part' || data.type === 'message.part.updated') && data.properties) {
00653| 		const part = data.properties.part;
00654| 		// sessionID is nested inside the part object
00655| 		const sessionID = part?.sessionID;

00657| 		if (sessionID && part && part.type) {

00692| 				const pendingClear = processingClearTimers.get(sessionID);
00693| 				if (pendingClear) {
00694| 					clearTimeout(pendingClear);
00695| 					processingClearTimers.delete(sessionID);
00696| 				}
00697| 				
00698| 				agents.update((agentList) => {
00699| 					return agentList.map((agent) => {
00700| 						if (agent.session_id === sessionID && agent.status === 'active') {
00701| 							return { ...agent, is_processing: true };
00702| 						}
00703| 						return agent;
00704| 					});
00705| 				});
00706| 			} else {

00708| 				// Cancel any existing timer first
00709| 				const existingTimer = processingClearTimers.get(sessionID);
00710| 				if (existingTimer) {
00711| 					clearTimeout(existingTimer);
00712| 				}
00713| 				
00714| 				// Set new timer for delayed clear
00715| 				const timer = setTimeout(() => {
00716| 					processingClearTimers.delete(sessionID);

00718| 						return agentList.map((agent) => {
00719| 							if (agent.session_id === sessionID) {
00720| 								return {
00721| 									...agent,
00722| 									is_processing: false
00723| 									// Note: Don't clear current_activity here - the last activity
00724| 									// should persist so users can see what the agent was doing.
00725| 									// Clearing it causes "Starting up..." to show for completed agents.
00726| 								};
00727| 							}
00728| 							return agent;
00729| 						});
00730| 					});
00731| 				}, PROCESSING_CLEAR_DELAY_MS);

00733| 				processingClearTimers.set(sessionID, timer);
00734| 			}
00735| 		}
00736| 	}

00738| 	// Handle lifecycle events - refresh agent list (debounced to prevent race conditions)
00739| 	// Note: session.status is NOT included because it's already handled via local state

00741| 	// session.status fires on every busy/idle toggle (high frequency).

Perfect! Now I'm getting a clear picture. I can see:

1. The agent card shows a "Processing" tooltip at line 464-466
2. The display state is computed based on `is_processing` flag
3. The `is_processing` is updated via SSE events from session.status and message.part events
4. The current status just shows whether the agent is "processing" or not, but not WHAT it's doing

From the requirement:
- Target: Show "Hatching... (thought for 8s)" or "Running Bash..." or "Reading files..." instead of just "Processing"

Looking at the SSE handling code (agents.ts:648-778), I can see that:
- `message.part` events contain the tool being used (line 757-761: "Using ${part.tool}")
- `current_activity` already captures this information (type, text, timestamp)
- The activity is displayed in the agent card at line 690-691

So the infrastructure is ALREADY THERE to show what the agent is doing! The issue is that:
1. The tooltip at line 464-466 just says "Processing" without showing what activity
2. We need to extract thinking duration
3. We need to show more expressive status like "Hatching..." for thinking

## Findings

### Finding 1: Current Activity Infrastructure Already Exists

**Evidence:** The `current_activity` field on Agent already captures type, text, and timestamp (web/src/lib/stores/agents.ts:62-67). SSE events from OpenCode populate this with tool names like "Using bash", "Reading files", etc. (agents.ts:754-778). The agent card displays this activity text at line 690-691.

**Source:** 
- web/src/lib/stores/agents.ts:62-67 (current_activity interface)
- web/src/lib/stores/agents.ts:648-778 (SSE event handlers)
- web/src/lib/components/agent-card/agent-card.svelte:687-696 (activity display)

**Significance:** We don't need to build new infrastructure - the backend already sends activity data via SSE. We just need to enhance the display to be more expressive.

---

### Finding 2: "Processing" Tooltip is Generic

**Evidence:** When `displayState === 'running'`, the tooltip shows "Processing" with subtext "Agent is actively generating a response" (agent-card.svelte:458-467). This doesn't leverage the `current_activity` data that's already available.

**Source:** 
- web/src/lib/components/agent-card/agent-card.svelte:458-467

**Significance:** The tooltip area is the perfect place to show expressive status. We should show the current activity instead of generic "Processing".

---

### Finding 3: Thinking Duration Not Captured

**Evidence:** While we track `current_activity.timestamp`, we don't specifically identify "thinking" vs "tool execution" phases. OpenCode sends `reasoning` type for thinking (agents.ts:772-774) but we don't calculate duration.

**Source:** 
- web/src/lib/stores/agents.ts:772-774 (reasoning type handling)

**Significance:** To show "Hatching... (thought for 8s)", we need to detect reasoning activity and calculate elapsed time since it started.

## TLDR

Enhanced agent status display to show expressive activity indicators ("Hatching... (thought for 8s)", "Running Bash...", "Reading files...") instead of generic "Processing" badge, leveraging existing SSE activity data infrastructure.

---

## Delta (What Changed)

### Files Created

### Files Modified
- `web/src/lib/components/agent-card/agent-card.svelte` - Added getExpressiveStatus() function to format activity into expressive status text, updated "Processing" tooltip to show contextual activity

### Commits
- `e00ae42b` - feat: add expressive agent status display

---

## Evidence (What Was Observed)

- Current activity infrastructure already exists: `current_activity` field captures type, text, and timestamp (agents.ts:62-67)
- SSE events from OpenCode populate activity with tool names ("Using bash", "Reading files") and reasoning events (agents.ts:754-778)
- "Processing" tooltip was generic and didn't leverage available activity data (agent-card.svelte:458-467)


### New Artifacts

### Decisions Made
- Decision 1: Use tooltip location for expressive status (most visible when agent is processing)
- Decision 2: Calculate thinking duration in real-time based on timestamp (rather than storing start times)
- Decision 3: Map tool names to friendly verbs ("Using bash" → "Running Bash...", "Using read" → "Reading files...")

### Key Insight
Backend already sends expressive activity data via SSE - frontend just wasn't displaying it expressively. This was a frontend display enhancement, not a new infrastructure build.

- Implementation follows established patterns (getActivityIcon, formatActivityAge)
- No new infrastructure - just display logic
- Orchestrator has working build environment
